name: Sheds
mission: The porch journal — reading sign, slope, and patience. Miles walked with empty hands still count. Wildlife ethics without trophy culture.
learnToday: Why deer drop antlers in late winter — nutrition, testosterone, and terrain
Reading south-facing slopes and bedding cover for shed probability
Ethics: do not disturb wintering wildlife, respect private land, leave habitat intact
How empty-handed walks still produce evidence: tracks, browse, rubs, and bedding sign
comingSoon: Slope and aspect shed-probability map (schematic, not GIS)
Season timing guide for Northeast PA
Sign identification lessons: rubs, scrapes, beds, tracks
Private shed journal with photograph evidence
Optional anonymous phenology notes — county level only

preview.title: The quiet search
preview.description: Shed Hunting teaches patience as a field skill. The tool will suggest where to walk based on slope, season, and habitat — not where someone else found antlers. Photograph sign, write what you learned, and honor the animals that left evidence behind.
